title: Emma's Big Feelings Day
synopsis: Emma experiences big emotions when her favorite stuffed elephant goes missing, learning that it's okay to feel scared and sad, and that asking for help can solve problems.

pages.text: Emma loved her stuffed elephant, Ellie. Every morning, Emma would hug Ellie tight and feel so happy. They played together, ate breakfast together, and went everywhere together.

illustration.description: A cheerful South Asian toddler girl with curly black hair in colorful pajamas sitting on a bright yellow bed, hugging a gray stuffed elephant with a big smile. Sunlight streams through a window with rainbow curtains. The room has warm orange walls and toys scattered around.
illustration.alt: Emma hugging her stuffed elephant Ellie on her bed

pages.text: But today, Ellie was gone! Emma looked under her blankets. She looked in her toy box. Where was Ellie? Emma's happy feelings turned into worried feelings.

illustration.description: Emma on her hands and knees looking under a rumpled bed with a concerned expression. Her dark eyes are wide with worry. The bedroom is messy with pillows and blankets everywhere. Bright blue and yellow toys are scattered on a red rug.
illustration.alt: Emma searching under her bed looking worried

pages.text: Emma felt scared without Ellie. Her tummy felt funny and her eyes filled with tears. 'Mama!' Emma called. 'I can't find Ellie anywhere!' Emma felt so sad and scared.

illustration.description: Emma standing in the middle of her colorful bedroom with tears in her eyes, arms outstretched toward the doorway. Her bottom lip quivers and her face shows genuine sadness. The room feels bigger around her small figure, emphasizing her vulnerability.
illustration.alt: Emma crying and calling for her mama

pages.text: Mama came running. She was a tall woman with kind brown eyes and long black hair. She knelt down and gave Emma a big, warm hug. 'It's okay to feel scared, sweetie. Let's find Ellie together.'

illustration.description: A loving South Asian mother with long black hair in a bright purple sweater kneeling down to hug Emma. Both have the same warm brown skin tone. The mother's face shows understanding and comfort. The background is soft yellow with heart-shaped decorations on the walls.
illustration.alt: Mama hugging Emma to comfort her

pages.text: Mama and Emma searched everywhere. They looked in the kitchen, behind the couch, and in the laundry basket. Finally, Papa found Ellie in the car! She had fallen under the seat.

illustration.description: Papa, a bearded man with glasses and a green shirt, reaching into a bright red car and pulling out the gray stuffed elephant. Emma and Mama watch excitedly from behind him. The garage has tools hanging on blue walls and afternoon light coming in.
illustration.alt: Papa finding Ellie in the car while Emma and Mama watch

pages.text: Emma felt so happy and brave! She hugged Ellie tight and thanked Mama and Papa. 'When I feel scared, I can ask for help,' Emma said. 'And my big feelings are okay too.'

illustration.description: Emma beaming with joy, holding Ellie close to her chest with both arms. Mama and Papa stand beside her with proud smiles. The family is surrounded by warm golden light. Emma's face radiates happiness and confidence, showing her emotional growth.
illustration.alt: Happy Emma hugging Ellie with her proud parents nearby

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Emma feel when she couldn't find Ellie?
questions.explanation: Emma felt scared and sad when Ellie was missing. These are big feelings that many children have!

questions.question: What did Emma do when she felt scared?
questions.explanation: Emma called for Mama when she felt scared. Asking for help is a good thing to do!

questions.question: Where did they find Ellie?
questions.explanation: Papa found Ellie in the car under the seat. The whole family helped look for her!

questions.question: What did Emma learn about her big feelings?
questions.explanation: Emma learned that big feelings are okay and that she can ask for help when she needs it.

pages.title: More Missing Words
pages.instructions: Complete the ending of Emma's story!
